food_subgroup: Fried or baked savoury snacks on stick

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for a fried seafood wrap on stick. FOODON_03315091 (seafood product) is the closest defensible parent category.
ontology.foodex2_description: Crustaceans and molluscs and other seafood products — processed seafood
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0CKM (processed seafood products) used as closest parent; no specific code exists for a fried seafood wrap on stick product.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No suitable SNOMED CT food concept found for this specific fried seafood wrap snack product.

main_ingredients: seafood filling (fish paste, prawns or crab meat)

serving.natural_language_portion: One typical serving is one piece on a stick, weighing approximately 65 g, as sold by Old Chang Kee at SGD 2.60 per piece.

nutrient_source.title: Old Chang Kee Seafood Roll / Fried Seafood Snack — estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy: Fish cake, fried (composite) and analogous fried seafood pastry snack data; cross-referenced with HPB Singapore Foodo fried snack category values
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service FoodData Central SR Legacy; Health Promotion Board Singapore (HPB Foodo)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact entry for 'Seafood Wrap OnStik' exists in HPB Foodo or USDA FoodData Central. Values are estimated by compositing USDA SR Legacy data for fried fish cake / fish paste products (FDC ID 175168 and related entries) with HPB Foodo fried snack pastry data. Serving size of 65 g is based on typical Old Chang Kee on-stick snack weight. All values are estimates and should be replaced with manufacturer-provided data when available.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of fried fish cake and analogous fried seafood-pastry snacks — estimated from University of Sydney GI Database entries for fried fish products and wheat-flour-based fried snacks
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Old Chang Kee Seafood Wrap OnStik. GI of 55 is estimated from University of Sydney GI Database analogous entries for fried fish cake (GI ~50–55) and wheat-flour fried snack pastry (GI ~55–60). The composite estimate of 55 (low category) is used. Glycemic load per serving calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (55 × 16.9) / 100 ≈ 9.3, where carbohydrate per 65 g serving = 26.0 × 0.65 = 16.9 g.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (480 mg per 100 g; approximately 312 mg per serving)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit consumption of this snack due to its high sodium content; one piece provides approximately 13% of the 2,400 mg daily sodium limit recommended by HPB Singapore, and frequent intake may contribute to elevated blood pressure.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate glycemic index (estimated GI 55) and moderate carbohydrate content from wheat flour pastry
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should consume this snack in moderation; the estimated glycemic load of approximately 9.3 per serving is moderate, and the refined wheat flour pastry component can cause a meaningful postprandial glucose rise, particularly if multiple pieces are consumed.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat from deep-frying and moderate cholesterol content
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals managing cardiovascular disease risk should limit intake of this deep-fried snack; each serving contributes approximately 2.1 g of saturated fat and 25 mg of cholesterol, and the high sodium content further elevates cardiovascular risk with regular consumption.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Seafood Wrap OnStik is a popular Singaporean savoury snack sold by Old Chang Kee, a well-known local chain specialising in curry puffs and on-stick fried snacks. The product consists of a seasoned seafood filling — typically made from fish paste, minced prawns, or a combination of seafood — encased in a thin wheat-flour pastry or batter coating, then deep-fried until golden and crispy, and served on a bamboo stick for easy eating on the go. The exterior is crunchy and lightly oily, while the interior is soft, moist, and savoury with a mild seafood flavour. It is priced at approximately SGD 2.60 per piece and is widely available at Old Chang Kee outlets across Singapore. Nutritionally, each 65 g piece provides an estimated 161 kcal, 6.2 g protein, 8.1 g fat, and 16.9 g carbohydrate. The sodium content is notable at approximately 312 mg per serving, making it a concern for individuals managing hypertension. The glycemic index is estimated at 55 (low), with a glycemic load of approximately 9.3 per serving, which is relevant for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes. The snack is not suitable for vegetarians, vegans, or those with fish, crustacean, or gluten allergies. It is generally considered halal. This snack is culturally embedded in Singapore's hawker and convenience snack culture and is commonly consumed as a mid-morning or afternoon snack, or as a light accompaniment to a meal.

confidence_reason: No exact nutritional entry for Old Chang Kee Seafood Wrap OnStik was found in HPB Foodo or USDA FoodData Central. Values are estimated from analogous fried seafood fish cake and fried pastry snack data from USDA SR Legacy and HPB Foodo category data. Confidence is medium pending manufacturer-provided nutritional data.
notes: Nutrient values are estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy fried fish cake / fish paste product entries and HPB Singapore Foodo fried snack category data; no exact manufacturer nutritional panel was available at time of processing. Serving size of 65 g is based on typical Old Chang Kee on-stick snack weight. Vitamin D is -1 as it is not reported in the analogous source data used for estimation. Vitamin E is -1 as it is not reported in the analogous source data used for estimation. Selenium is -1 as it is not reported in the analogous source data used for estimation. GI of 55 is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database entries for fried fish cake and wheat-flour fried snack pastry; no direct GI measurement exists for this product. All values should be replaced with manufacturer-provided data when available. The food is classified as a Singaporean snack under the curry puff / on-stick snack category as sold by Old Chang Kee.
